data.title: China's CSPC Pharmaceutical, AstraZeneca partner on long-acting obesity drugs
data.description:
• CSPC Pharmaceutical Group said on Friday it has signed an agreement with UK drugmaker AstraZeneca (AZN) to jointly develop innovative long-acting peptide therapies targeting obesity and related weight-management conditions.
  • For access to eight programs and related platforms, AstraZeneca (AZN) will pay the group an upfront $1.2 billion, with the potential for up to $3.5 billion in research and development milestones, up to $13.8 billion in sales-based milestone payments, and royalties of up to double-digit percentages tied to annual net sales of the licensed products.

data.title: Australia’s producer inflation cools to 0.8% in Q4, easing below market forecasts
data.description:
Australia's Producer Price Index ((PPI)) rose 0.8% in Q4 2025, down from 1.0% the previous quarter and below the expected 1.1% increase. This was the 22nd quarter of producer inflation, driven by strong demand in the residential property sector.

On an annual basis, producer prices climbed 3.5% in Q4, the same pace as in Q3.

Separate data showed Australia’s private sector credit rose 0.8% month-on-month in December 2025, exceeding market expectations of 0.6% and accelerating from a 0.6% growth in the previous month. 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.4% to around 8,960 in early Friday deals and is up 2.9% for the month. The Australian dollar traded around $0.701, hovering near a three-year high.

More on Australia:

data.title: Stryker projects 8%–9.5% organic sales growth for 2026 while advancing margin expansion and new product launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stryker Corporation (SYK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Kevin Lobo described 2025 as “outstanding for both Q4 and the full year across all key financial metrics,” with organic sales growth of 11% in Q4 and 10.3% for the year, surpassing $25 billion in sales. Lobo highlighted that Neurocranial, Endoscopy, Instruments, and Trauma and Extremities all delivered double-digit organic sales growth globally. He emphasized, “Full-year U.S. organic sales growth was an impressive 11.2%, and international organic sales growth of 7.5%,” citing emerging markets, South Korea, and Japan as key contributors.
  • Lobo outlined strategic moves including the creation of the SmartCare business unit, combining Vocera and care.ai, and the launch of a specialized breast care sales force within Endoscopy. He also noted the appointment of Spencer Stiles as President and Chief Operating Officer in December, “It provides him really a tremendous platform to lead our global commercial organization. It also enables a cascade of other promotions.”
  • CFO Preston Wells reported, “Our fourth quarter adjusted earnings per share of $4.47 was up 11.5% from the same quarter last year, driven by sales growth and operating margin expansion, partially offset by tariffs, higher interest expense and a higher effective tax rate.” He further noted, “Our margin expansion included improvements in gross margin from business mix and cost improvements despite the impact of tariffs.”

Outlook

  • Stryker projects 2026 organic net sales growth in the range of 8% to 9.5% and adjusted net earnings per share in the range of $14.90 to $15.10. CFO Wells stated, “Our full year 2026 sales guidance includes a modestly positive impact from price,” and pointed to a “slightly favorable impact” from foreign exchange if current rates hold. The company expects full year tariff impacts to be approximately $400 million, including an incremental $200 million compared to 2025, realized in the first half.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the guidance range for organic growth is slightly wider and higher at the top end. Lobo explained, “We really feel more positive, I'd say, modestly more positive this year than we did 1 year ago, which gives us the confidence to start the year with that range, a little wider range, but a little on the higher end.”

Financial Results

  • Organic sales growth for Q4 was 11%, compared to 10.2% in Q4 2024. The company reported that pricing and foreign currency both had a favorable impact on sales.
  • Adjusted earnings per share for the full year reached $13.63, up 11.8% from 2024. The company delivered free cash flow as a percentage of adjusted net earnings of 81% for the year.
  • MedSurg and Neurotechnology reported 12.6% organic sales growth in Q4, with 13% U.S. organic growth. Instruments posted 19.1% U.S. organic growth, and Endoscopy saw 11.1% U.S. organic growth. Medical achieved 13.6% U.S. organic sales growth.
  • In Orthopaedics, U.S. Knee business grew 7.6% organically, and Hips grew 5.6%. U.S. Other Ortho grew 28.7% organically, attributed to Mako 4 installations. Adjusted gross margin was 65.2%, 10 basis points lower than Q4 2024, while adjusted operating margin improved by 100 basis points.

Q&A

  • Larry Biegelsen (Wells Fargo): Asked about confidence in higher 2026 guidance and the President/COO appointment. Lobo replied, “Given the order book, given the strength of the Mako performance... we really feel more positive.” On leadership changes: “It provides him really a tremendous platform to lead our global commercial organization.”
  • Robert Marcus (JPMorgan): Asked about pricing for capital and implant businesses and capital environment. Wells responded, “We expect 2026 to look pretty similar from a price standpoint to 2025,” while Beach added, “We feel really good about the capital environment as we go into 2026.”
  • Analysts repeatedly queried about growth drivers, margin expansion, innovation, Mako platform durability, price dynamics, and international growth prospects.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was positive, with repeated congratulations and questions focused on sustaining strong growth, pricing, and innovation. Queries reflected interest in durability of growth and new product impact.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing structural strengths and strong momentum. During Q&A, Lobo and Wells remained upbeat, reinforcing guidance confidence and operational discipline. Leadership was forthcoming about challenges but highlighted mitigation and future opportunities.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analyst tone were more optimistic, with an emphasis on momentum and positive expectations for 2026.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw an increase in guidance for 2026 compared to the previous quarter’s outlook for 2025. Management expanded its strategic focus, particularly with new business units and leadership changes.
  • Financial results reflected higher organic growth rates and margin expansion compared to Q3. Analysts’ questions shifted from concerns about supply chain issues and integration (notably Inari) to growth sustainability and innovation.
  • Management confidence appears elevated, highlighted by Lobo’s remarks on “momentum entering 2026.” Analyst sentiment moved from neutral/positive to more consistently positive, focusing on upside potential.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariff headwinds, with $400 million anticipated impact in 2026, $200 million more than in 2025, to be realized in the first half. Wells noted, “That is reflective of the annualization really of all the work and activity that's been done.”
  • Competitive pressures were noted in the ischemic vascular business, though partially offset by new product launches like the Surpass Elite stent.
  • International growth is somewhat constrained by regulatory delays in Europe, although management expects improvement if MDR reforms proceed. Lobo stated, “Europe has woken up to the reality that they are stunting innovation and not giving patients access to products in a timely manner.”

Final Takeaway

Stryker’s 2025 performance set new records across sales, margins, and cash flow, driven by robust demand and operational execution. The company is guiding for continued high growth in 2026, supported by a strong order book, new product launches, and strategic leadership changes. Management remains confident in overcoming tariff challenges and capitalizing on international opportunities, with a focus on sustaining margin expansion and market leadership in MedSurg, Orthopaedics, and technology platforms.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Japan economic data: Retail sales miss, industrial production beats, and unemployment holds steady
data.description:
Japan’s economy faces a mixed outlook as 2025 concludes, highlighted by an unexpected 0.9% year-on-year drop in retail sales and a second consecutive month of slight industrial production declines.

Retail sales in Japan fell by 0.9% year-on-year in December 2025, marking the first drop since August and coming after a 1.1% increase in November. This decline was unexpected and reflected that households had met year-end spending needs earlier, despite rising wages. On a monthly basis, retail sales also dropped by 2.0% in December after a 0.7% gain in November. 

Industrial production decreased by 0.1% month-over-month in December, a smaller drop than the previous month's 2.7% and less than the expected 0.4% decline. However, it was the second consecutive month of decline. On an annual basis, production grew by 2.6%, the strongest in three months. 

The unemployment rate remained steady at 2.6%, with the number of unemployed increasing by 50 thousand to a total of 1.86 million, which is the highest in 17 months. Meanwhile, employment fell by 50 thousand to 68.46 million, while the labor force dropped 50 thousand to 70.31 million. 

Core consumer prices in Tokyo rose by 2% year-on-year in January 2026, down from 2.3% the previous month and below expectations.

The Nikkei 225 Index rose 0.3% to around 53,480 on Friday, extending gains for a fourth consecutive session, while the broader Topix Index advanced 0.5% to 3,563. The Japanese yen slipped toward 154 per dollar on Friday but remained on track for its first monthly gain since August, as talks of intervention pushed the currency to four-month highs.

More on Japan's economy:

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM PPI-Final Demand

The Producer Price Index (PPI) of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) is a family of indexes that measures the average change over time in the prices received by domestic producers of goods and services.

9:45 AM Chicago PMI

The Institute for Supply Management - Chicago compiles a survey and a composite diffusion index of business conditions in the Chicago area.

Not much change is expected with the index at 43.8 in January versus 43.5 in December.

1:30 PM Alberto Musalem Speaks 

St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem speaks on the U.S. economy and monetary policy and participates in a moderated discussion before the University of Arkansas Center for Business and Economic Research 32nd Annual Business Forecast Luncheon.

3:00 PM Farm Prices

The Department of Agriculture releases at the end of the month the index of prices received by farmers for the preceding month.

data.title: Blackstone is said to consider selling Interplex’s $1 billion ICT unit - report
data.description:
• Blackstone (BX) is considering selling Interplex’s information and communications technology unit amid interest from other private equity firms and industry players, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Blackstone is working with a financial adviser on a potential sale, the people said. A transaction might value the ICT business at more than $1 billion, the report said.

data.title: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf gets a 28% pay raise to $40M
Microsoft reportedly inks $750 million cloud deal with AI firm Perplexity
data.description:
• AI startup Perplexity is said to have signed a $750 million deal with Microsoft (MSFT) to use its Azure cloud service, spreading its business beyond longtime cloud partner Amazon.com (AMZN).
  • The three-year commitment will let Perplexity deploy AI models through Microsoft’s Foundry service, including those made by OpenAI (OPENAI), Anthropic, and xAI, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the deal.
  • "We are excited to partner with Microsoft for access to frontier models from X, OpenAI, and Anthropic,” a Perplexity spokesperson said, according to the report.
  • Perplexity hasn’t shifted spending from Amazon Web Services, long the startup’s main cloud provider, as part of the Microsoft deal, the spokesperson added.

data.title: First Internet Bancorp outlines 15%-17% loan growth and higher provision for credit losses in 2026 amid fintech expansion and targeted credit clean-up
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: First Internet Bancorp (INBK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • David Becker, Chairman & CEO, stated, "We are pleased to close 2025 with strong fourth quarter results that demonstrate the power of our differentiated digital banking model. Our core business fundamentals remain robust with quarterly revenue up 21% over the prior year period." Becker highlighted a 30% net interest income increase year-over-year, consistent net interest margin expansion, and disciplined expense management. He also referenced the strategic sale of approximately $850 million in single-tenant lease financing loans to Blackstone, stating it "strengthened our capital position, enhanced our rate risk profile and accelerated our progress towards achieving a 1% return on average assets."

  • Becker remarked on Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS) growth, with over $1.3 billion in new deposits for 2025 and processing of $165 billion in payments, marking a 225% increase from 2024. He emphasized that "these partnerships have evolved to become true strategic revenue drivers through reoccurring transaction fees, program management fees and interest income." He further addressed credit challenges, noting, "our credit issues are isolated to 2 specific portfolios, SBA and franchise finance," and guided to a higher provision for credit losses in 2026 than initially estimated.

  • Nicole Lorch, President, COO & Corporate Secretary, discussed the SBA segment, noting $8.6 million in secondary market sales for SBA loans through November and December despite a prolonged government shutdown. Lorch stated, "we are strategically realigning our SBA production with our enhanced and more stringent underwriting guidelines," targeting $500 million in originations for 2026 and providing estimated gain on sale revenue of $19 million to $20 million, down from $29.4 million in 2025. She highlighted the bank's BaaS platform, saying, "our payment processing volumes continue to reach impressive scale. We facilitated $65 billion in payments for our fintech partners in the fourth quarter, which was up over 40% from volumes processed in the third quarter."

  • Kenneth Lovik, Executive VP & CFO, reported, "We delivered solid fourth quarter results with net income of $5.3 million or $0.60 per diluted share." He added, "adjusted total revenue for the quarter was $42.1 million, a 21% increase over the fourth quarter of 2024." Lovik noted net interest margin improved to 2.22%, and that "funding costs during the same period... fell to 3.68% from 4.30% in the prior year period."

Outlook

  • Lovik provided guidance, stating, "We expect continued loan growth in the range of 15% to 17%, driven by strong pipelines across our commercial lending verticals as well as a lower base coming off the balance sheet repositioning trade in the third quarter." He projected net interest margin expansion to 2.75% to 2.80% by Q4 2026, fully taxable equivalent net interest income of $155 million to $160 million, and noninterest income of $33 million to $35 million. Operating expenses are projected at $111 million to $112 million. Provision for credit losses is estimated at $50 million to $53 million, with first quarter provision expected in the range of $17 million to $19 million. This translates to earnings per share guidance of $2.35 to $2.45 for 2026. Management expects credit to improve gradually in the second half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Net income for the quarter was $5.3 million, or $0.60 per diluted share. Adjusted net income excluding the loan sale impact was $5.6 million, with adjusted EPS of $0.64. Adjusted total revenue was $42.1 million, and adjusted pre-provision net revenue totaled $17.9 million, up 66% year-over-year. Net interest income was $30.3 million, and net interest margin improved to 2.22%. Total loans as of December 31, 2025, were $3.7 billion, up $143 million from the linked quarter. Total deposits were $4.8 billion. Provision for credit losses in Q4 was $12 million, with $16 million in net charge-offs. Noninterest expense for the quarter totaled $24.2 million.

Q&A

  • Brett Rabatin, Hovde Group, asked about the SBA balance sheet composition, loan yield, and CD repricing. Kenneth Lovik responded that deposit costs are expected to continue declining, with a notable pickup from CD maturities and fintech deposits, and that "all the new yields coming on the balance sheet are just obviously higher than what the current yield is in the portfolio today."

  • Rabatin inquired about criticized loans and credit migration. Lovik indicated criticized loans increased by about $16 million, predominantly in SBA, but mostly in the special mention category. Becker explained most SBA issues arise in the 12- to 18-month window, and the team has reached out to over 400 borrowers for proactive engagement.

  • Nathan Race, Piper Sandler, asked about interest reversals and provision expectations. Lovik modeled interest reversals into net interest income, with $300,000 to $400,000 of reversals in the quarter. On provision, about half is for charge-offs and half for specific reserves, with expectations for allowance growth by $20 million to $30 million by year-end.

  • George Sutton, Craig-Hallum, questioned BaaS income and M&A interest. Lovik confirmed fintech income is broken out in noninterest income, and Becker disclosed the company has been approached by several potential acquirers but has no deals imminent.

  • Emily Lee, KBW, asked about fintech deposit growth and earnings impact. Lovik said most deposit growth is from existing fintech partners, and fintech revenue, including interest income from lending partnerships, totaled $6.7 million, more than double last year.

  • Race followed up on deposit growth funding loan growth. Lovik expects deposit growth of 8% to 10%, with flexibility from existing cash and off-balance-sheet fintech deposits.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on credit migration, criticized loans, and provision guidance, reflecting a slightly negative tone and persistent focus on asset quality and earnings impacts.

  • Management maintained confidence in core revenue growth and risk management initiatives, using phrases like "We remain confident in our ability to deliver strong financial performance while building long-term shareholder value."

  • Compared to last quarter, analysts are more focused on the sustainability of asset quality improvements, while management has shifted from emphasizing portfolio clean-up to highlighting future credit stabilization and technology-enabled risk management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 provision for credit losses has increased from prior expectations, signaling a more cautious approach to credit costs. Loan growth guidance has risen to 15%-17%, up from the focus on commercial pipeline strength last quarter. Management tone shifted from addressing acute credit clean-up to a more forward-looking emphasis on stabilization and technology investments. Analysts' questions this quarter focused more on credit migration and fintech revenue visibility, in contrast to last quarter’s emphasis on loan sales and capital flexibility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited isolated credit issues in SBA and franchise finance portfolios, with a higher provision for credit losses guided for 2026. Enhanced risk management and AI-driven analytics are being deployed to mitigate future risks. Analysts raised concerns about criticized loans, interest reversals, and the timing of credit stabilization.

Final Takeaway

First Internet Bancorp concluded the year with strong revenue growth and operational momentum, underscored by significant fintech-driven deposit and payment processing expansion. Management is proactively addressing elevated credit costs in specific portfolios, with expectations for gradual credit improvement in the back half of 2026 as problem loans are resolved. The company projects robust loan growth and net interest margin expansion, while continued investment in technology and disciplined capital management are expected to enhance long-term profitability and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. to discuss rare earth price mechanism with allies next week - report
data.description:
The U.S. will seek to reach consensus with allies on a pricing mechanism to help insulate rare earth mineral refiners and extractors during meetings with dozens of foreign ministers in Washington next week, a senior Trump administration official told Bloomberg News.

In an interview on Thursday, US Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs Jacob Helberg said he expects a lot of “momentum and excitement” toward “agreeing on a pricing mechanism that we can all coordinate together on in order to ensure price stability for people in the mineral refining and extraction business.”

The move follows a late-Wednesday selloff in rare earth stocks after Reuters reported that the Trump administration had stepped back from plans to guarantee minimum prices for U.S. critical minerals projects.

U.S. mining and processing companies have pushed for price floors and other government backstops to help them compete with China, whose state-backed producers can cut prices to punish rivals, undercut projects, and deter private investment.

Relevant stocks include USA Rare Earth (USAR), United States Antimony (UAMY), Critical Metals (CRML), Ramaco Resources (METC), NioCorp Developments (NB), MP Materials (MP), TMC the metals company (TMC), Lithium Americas (LAC), Perpetua Resources (PPTA), Nouveau Monde Graphite (NMG), Trilogy Metals (TMQ), Westwater Resources (WWR), Albemarle (ALB), Sigma Lithium (SGML), and Standard Lithium (SLI).

ETFs: (REMX), (XME), (LIT), (BATT)
